examples.sentence: I think I'll give the party a miss tonight; I've got too much work to do.
examples.translation: I think I'll deliberately avoid the party tonight; I've got too much work to do.

examples.sentence: She gave the meeting a miss last week because she was feeling unwell.
examples.translation: She deliberately skipped the meeting last week because she was feeling unwell.

examples.sentence: If you don't feel like going to the concert, just give it a miss.
examples.translation: If you don't want to attend the concert, just choose not to participate.

examples.sentence: They gave the movie a miss since all the reviews were negative.
examples.translation: They deliberately avoided watching the movie since all the reviews were negative.

examples.sentence: I've never cooked Indian food before, but I'll give it a try this weekend.
examples.translation: I've never cooked Indian food before, but I'll attempt it this weekend.

examples.sentence: Why not give skydiving a try? It could be an unforgettable experience.
examples.translation: Why not attempt skydiving? It could be an unforgettable experience.

examples.sentence: After hearing so many good things about the new app, Sarah decided to give it a try.
examples.translation: After hearing many good things about the new app, Sarah decided to attempt using it.

examples.sentence: John is going to give the new software a try before deciding to buy it.
examples.translation: John is going to attempt using the new software before deciding to buy it.

examples.sentence: If you give this exercise a try, you might find it easier than you expected.
examples.translation: If you attempt this exercise, you might find it easier than you expected.

examples.sentence: They gave the prototype a try to see if it would function correctly.
examples.translation: They attempted to use the prototype to check if it would work properly.

examples.sentence: We will give the new recipe a try tonight and see how it turns out.
examples.translation: We will attempt to cook the new recipe tonight and see the result.

examples.sentence: He gave the wheel a whirl to check if the bearings were still smooth.
examples.translation: He spun the wheel quickly to check if the bearings were still smooth.

examples.sentence: Can you give the top a whirl and see how long it spins?
examples.translation: Can you spin the top quickly and see how long it spins?

examples.sentence: The mechanic gave the pulley a whirl before reinstalling it.
examples.translation: The mechanic rotated the pulley by giving it a quick turn before reinstalling it.

examples.sentence: She gave the doorknob a whirl, but it was stuck and wouldn’t turn.
examples.translation: She quickly turned the doorknob, but it was stuck and wouldn’t rotate.

examples.sentence: I've never painted before, but I'm willing to give it a whirl this weekend.
examples.translation: I've never painted before, but I want to try it as an experiment this weekend.

examples.sentence: Why don’t you give salsa dancing a whirl? It’s a lot of fun even if you’re a beginner.
examples.translation: Why don’t you try salsa dancing? It’s enjoyable even without serious commitment.

examples.sentence: They decided to give the new board game a whirl to see if it would be a hit at parties.
examples.translation: They chose to try the new board game for fun to see if it would be popular at parties.

examples.sentence: The guide gave the axe to the lumberjack before they started chopping the trees.
examples.translation: The guide handed the axe to the lumberjack before they began chopping the trees.

examples.sentence: She was given the axe carefully to avoid any accidents during the camping trip.
examples.translation: She was handed the axe carefully to prevent accidents during the camping trip.

examples.sentence: Can you give him the axe? He needs it to split the firewood.
examples.translation: Can you hand him the axe? He requires it to split the firewood.

examples.sentence: They were given the axes at the start of the forest clearing exercise.
examples.translation: They received the axes at the beginning of the forest clearing activity.

examples.sentence: After months of poor performance, the company finally gave her the axe.
examples.translation: After months of poor performance, the company finally fired her.

examples.sentence: If the budget cuts continue, they will have to give the axe to several staff members.
examples.translation: If the budget cuts persist, they will have to dismiss several employees.

examples.sentence: He was given the axe without any warning, which left him shocked and confused.
examples.translation: He was fired without any warning, leaving him shocked and confused.

examples.sentence: Give the devil his due, Marcus knows how to get things done even if he can be difficult.
examples.translation: Acknowledge Marcus’s merits even though he is difficult.

examples.sentence: Though she's often harsh, we must give the devil his due and admit she’s very effective.
examples.translation: Even though she is harsh, we must concede her effectiveness.

examples.sentence: You have to give the devil his due; despite the criticism, the author writes compelling stories.
examples.translation: You need to acknowledge the author's merits despite criticisms.

examples.sentence: Give the devil his due: even his opponents admit he has a sharp mind.
examples.translation: Concede that even his opponents admit he has intelligence.

examples.sentence: We might dislike him, but giving the devil his due means recognizing his contributions.
examples.translation: Though we dislike him, we must admit his contributions.

examples.sentence: Let’s give the devil his due and acknowledge that the plan has some merit.
examples.translation: Let's concede that the plan has some merit.

examples.sentence: Despite his flaws, they gave the devil his due and recognized his efforts.
examples.translation: Despite flaws, they acknowledged his positive efforts.

examples.sentence: The critics gave the devil his due by praising the artist’s technical skill even if they disliked his style.
examples.translation: Critics acknowledged the artist’s skill despite disliking his style.

examples.sentence: She gave the devil his due, admitting that her competitor had some good ideas.
examples.translation: She conceded that her competitor had good ideas.

examples.sentence: Even though he was unpopular, the team gave the devil his due and respected his dedication.
examples.translation: Although unpopular, the team acknowledged his dedication.

examples.sentence: During the crowded concert, he accidentally gave me the elbow while trying to get through.
examples.translation: During the crowded concert, he touched me with his elbow accidentally while passing.

examples.sentence: She gave her friend the elbow to get his attention across the noisy room.
examples.translation: She pushed her friend gently with her elbow to get his attention.

examples.sentence: I was given the elbow by my teammate during the game when we collided unexpectedly.
examples.translation: My teammate accidentally struck me with his elbow during the game.

examples.sentence: He gave the waiter the elbow to discreetly signal he wanted the bill.
examples.translation: He touched the waiter with his elbow to silently ask for the bill.

examples.sentence: After the company restructured, many employees were given the elbow without much notice.
examples.translation: After restructuring, many employees were fired unexpectedly.

examples.sentence: If sales don’t improve, they will give the elbow to the whole sales team.
examples.translation: If sales don’t improve, the entire sales team will be dismissed.

examples.sentence: He was given the elbow after only six months on the job, which was quite surprising.
examples.translation: He was fired after six months, which was surprising.

examples.sentence: After realizing they wanted different things, she gave him the elbow to end the relationship.
examples.translation: She ended the relationship by breaking up with him.

examples.sentence: He was given the elbow when his girlfriend decided to move to another city.
examples.translation: His girlfriend broke up with him when she moved away.

examples.sentence: Don’t be upset if she gives you the elbow; sometimes people just grow apart.
examples.translation: Don’t be upset if she breaks up with you; sometimes people grow apart.

examples.sentence: He tried to join their group, but they gave him the elbow and ignored his presence.
examples.translation: He was rejected socially and ignored by the group.

examples.sentence: Despite his efforts to make friends, the clique gave him the elbow every time.
examples.translation: The clique consistently rebuffed him socially.

examples.sentence: When Laura asked to join the conversation, she was given the elbow by the others.
examples.translation: Laura was socially rebuffed when she tried to join the conversation.

examples.sentence: At the end of the card game, the dealer gave the game away by announcing the winner.
examples.translation: At the end of the card game, the dealer revealed the outcome by announcing the winner.

examples.sentence: He carefully gave the game away by handing over the prize to the champion.
examples.translation: He revealed the contest result by giving the prize to the winner.

examples.sentence: Before the contest ended, she accidentally gave the game away by showing her cards early.
examples.translation: She revealed the game's outcome prematurely by exposing her cards.

examples.sentence: The referee gave the game away by signaling the final score to the crowd.
examples.translation: The referee revealed the game's result by signaling the final score.

examples.sentence: I didn’t mean to give the game away, but I accidentally mentioned the surprise party.
examples.translation: I unintentionally revealed the secret about the surprise party.

examples.sentence: She gave the game away when she laughed before the joke was finished.
examples.translation: She revealed the secret by laughing prematurely.

examples.sentence: Try not to give the game away about the new project; management wants it kept quiet.
examples.translation: Avoid revealing the secret about the new project; management wants it confidential.

examples.sentence: During the debate, he gave the lie to his opponent’s claim about the budget cuts.
examples.translation: During the debate, he accused his opponent directly of lying about the budget cuts.

examples.sentence: She gave the lie to the rumor by showing proof that she was out of town that day.
examples.translation: She directly told the rumor was false by providing alibi evidence.

examples.sentence: No one dared to give the lie to the mayor’s statement during the public forum.
examples.translation: No one challenged the mayor’s statement by accusing him of lying.

examples.sentence: The new evidence gave the lie to the defendant’s story and led to a conviction.
examples.translation: The new evidence proved the defendant’s story was false.

examples.sentence: His actions gave the lie to his promises of reform.
examples.translation: His actions showed that his promises of reform were untrue.

examples.sentence: The report gave the lie to earlier claims that the project was on schedule.
examples.translation: The report demonstrated that the earlier claims about the project schedule were false.

examples.sentence: He was furious when she gave him the lie during their argument.
examples.translation: He was angry when she directly called him a liar during their argument.

examples.sentence: They gave the lie to the witness’s testimony in court.
examples.translation: They directly accused the witness of lying in court.

examples.sentence: If you give the lie to his explanation, be prepared for a heated response.
examples.translation: If you accuse him of lying, be ready for a strong reaction.

examples.sentence: She was not afraid to give the lie to false accusations made against her.
examples.translation: She was unafraid to directly call false accusations against her untrue.

examples.sentence: I had to give the lie to his story because the facts didn’t add up.
examples.translation: I told him he was lying because the facts were inconsistent.

examples.sentence: She gave the lie to the report by presenting her own evidence.
examples.translation: She accused the report of being false by showing her own evidence.

examples.sentence: When the politician made false claims, the journalist was quick to give the lie to them.
examples.translation: The journalist promptly told the politician he was lying about his claims.

examples.sentence: The new data gives the lie to the theory that the earth is flat.
examples.translation: The new data disproves the theory that the earth is flat.

examples.sentence: His calm demeanor gives the lie to the rumors that he’s nervous about the presentation.
examples.translation: His calmness shows that the rumors about his nervousness are false.

examples.sentence: The investigation gave the lie to the accusations of corruption within the department.
examples.translation: The investigation disproved the accusations of corruption in the department.

examples.sentence: She was not afraid to give the lie to anyone spreading false information.
examples.translation: She was not afraid to tell people directly that they were lying.

examples.sentence: During the meeting, he gave the lie to the inaccurate figures presented.
examples.translation: He told them directly that the figures presented were false.

examples.sentence: If you give the lie to his explanation, you must be ready to provide proof.
examples.translation: If you tell him he is lying, you need to have evidence.

examples.sentence: They gave the lie to the statement by confronting the speaker openly.
examples.translation: They directly told the speaker he was lying by confronting him openly.

examples.sentence: When Sarah visited, we gave her the royal treatment with a fancy dinner and spa day.
examples.translation: When Sarah visited, we treated her with exceptional kindness and luxury.

examples.sentence: The hotel staff gave the guests the royal treatment, making sure every need was met.
examples.translation: The hotel staff treated the guests with exceptional attention and care.

examples.sentence: If you want to impress your in-laws, give them the royal treatment during their stay.
examples.translation: To impress your in-laws, treat them with exceptional kindness and attention.

examples.sentence: The ceremony was grand as they gave the royal treatment to the visiting dignitaries.
examples.translation: The ceremony provided honors appropriate to actual royalty for the dignitaries.

examples.sentence: They gave the royal treatment by arranging a formal reception fit for the queen.
examples.translation: They provided ceremonies and honors suitable for a queen.

examples.sentence: During the state visit, the ambassador was given the royal treatment with full honors.
examples.translation: The ambassador received ceremonies and services appropriate to royalty.

examples.sentence: The palace staff gave the royal treatment by preparing a lavish banquet for the king.
examples.translation: The palace staff organized a luxurious banquet in accordance with royal standards.

examples.sentence: After repeated mistakes, the manager gave the sack to the underperforming employee.
examples.translation: The manager dismissed the employee due to repeated mistakes.

examples.sentence: They will give the sack to anyone caught breaching company policy.
examples.translation: They will terminate employment for anyone breaking company rules.

examples.sentence: I heard that the company gave the sack to half the marketing team last month.
examples.translation: I heard the company fired half the marketing team last month.

examples.sentence: If you continue being late, you might get the sack soon.
examples.translation: If you keep being late, you might be dismissed from your job.

examples.sentence: When asked, he gave me the time of day and said it was quarter past three.
examples.translation: He told me the current time was 3:15.

examples.sentence: Can you give the time of day? My watch stopped working.
examples.translation: Can you tell me the current time? My watch is broken.

examples.sentence: She always gives the time of day to customers who ask politely.
examples.translation: She tells the current time to customers who ask nicely.

examples.sentence: They gave the time of day to all the participants before the race started.
examples.translation: They informed all participants of the current time before the race.

examples.sentence: Despite their past disagreements, he finally gave her the time of day at the reunion.
examples.translation: He acknowledged and showed respect to her at the reunion.

examples.sentence: I tried to talk to him, but he wouldn’t even give me the time of day.
examples.translation: I tried to get his attention, but he refused to acknowledge me.

examples.sentence: It's frustrating when your boss doesn't give you the time of day, even when you have good ideas.
examples.translation: It's frustrating when your boss ignores or doesn't acknowledge you despite your ideas.

examples.sentence: After the argument, she refused to give him the time of day at the office.
examples.translation: After the argument, she ignored him at work.

examples.sentence: He felt snubbed because his classmates wouldn’t give him the time of day.
examples.translation: He felt rejected because his classmates ignored him.

examples.sentence: Don’t expect them to give you the time of day after what happened last week.
examples.translation: Don’t expect them to acknowledge you after last week’s event.

examples.sentence: Sadly, the old dog finally gave up the ghost after many happy years.
examples.translation: Sadly, the old dog died after many happy years.

examples.sentence: The ancient tree gave up the ghost during last winter’s storm.
examples.translation: The ancient tree died during last winter’s storm.

examples.sentence: When the elderly woman gave up the ghost, the whole village mourned her loss.
examples.translation: When the elderly woman died, the entire village mourned.

examples.sentence: Many pets give up the ghost quietly, surrounded by their owners’ love.
examples.translation: Many pets die peacefully while being cared for by their owners.

examples.sentence: My laptop gave up the ghost just before the deadline, and I lost all my work.
examples.translation: My laptop stopped functioning just before the deadline.

examples.sentence: The old car gave up the ghost on the highway, forcing us to call for help.
examples.translation: The old car ceased operating on the highway, so we had to call for assistance.

examples.sentence: If the printer gives up the ghost again, we’ll need to buy a new one.
examples.translation: If the printer stops working again, we’ll have to purchase a new one.
